title: Whole-cell Biosensor Design
description: A living biosensor: a safe bacterial chassis engineered with a CRISPR-based sensing circuit that detects an input analyte and produces a readout. The design references a chassis from the safe-chassis catalog (BSL-1/GRAS only).

status.description: validated/published = real device from a cited source; template = real parts assembled into a CRISPR design pattern (not an end-to-end validated device); experimental/community = lab- or user-stage.

chassisSlug.description: References a chassis in the safe-chassis catalog (chassis.schema.json). Must resolve to a BSL-1 chassis.

designType.description: Circuit topology. single = one input -> reporter (default). AND/OR = multi-input logic gates. band-pass = responds only within a concentration window. memory = records a transient exposure. ratiometric = two reporters for quantitation.

detects.description: Plain-language description of the detected signal.

operatingRange.description: Concentration range the sensor responds over.

additionalInputs.description: Extra inputs for multi-input designs (AND/OR). The primary input stays in 'input'.

strategy.description: How CRISPR mediates sensing. CRISPRi-repression: dCas9+sgRNA represses the reporter; the analyte gates dCas9/sgRNA so the reporter inverts. CRISPRa: dCas9-activator turns the reporter ON.

sensorMechanism.description: How the analyte is first sensed, e.g. 'ArsR repressor releases Pars promoter on arsenite binding'.

signalFlow.description: End-to-end logic: analyte -> sensor -> CRISPR layer -> reporter ON/OFF.

parts.description: Genetic parts composing the sensing circuit, in transcriptional order where meaningful.

partId.description: Registry id if any, e.g. iGEM BBa_K..., SEVA, Addgene.

sequence.description: Nucleotide sequence (optional; include when from a cited source).

source.description: Where this part comes from (organism / paper / registry).

reporterGene.description: e.g. GFP, sfGFP, mCherry, amilCP (chromoprotein), luxCDABE, lacZ.

readout.description: How the result is observed, e.g. 'green fluorescence', 'visible blue pigment', 'smartphone camera'.

result.description: What a positive result looks like.

validated.description: Whether the integrated device (not just the parts) has been experimentally validated in a cited source.

biocontainment.description: Containment strategy for deployment, e.g. 'auxotrophy + kill-switch', 'lab-only'.

buildSteps.description: Ordered, human-readable steps to build and run the biosensor.

partsSources.description: Citations for the sensing parts when the integrated design is a template.
